title: Delicious Vietnamese Cashews Curry
description: A bitter, molten Vietnamese curry with cashews, coconut milk, and dark chocolate, made with a spice paste from the mortar and pestle.
instructions: Wash the cashews and soak them in warm water for 10 minutes.
Grind cumin, coriander, turmeric, and ginger in a mortar and pestle until a fine paste forms.
In a large saucepan, heat a tablespoon of oil, add the spice paste, and sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the sliced chili, tamarind paste, fish sauce, and water, stir to combine.
Pour in the coconut milk, bring to a gentle simmer, and cook for 5 minutes.
Drain the soaked cashews and add them to the curry, stirring to coat.
Reduce heat and let the curry simmer for 10 minutes until it thickens slightly.
Stir in the dark chocolate until it fully melts and the sauce becomes glossy and molten.
Season with salt to taste, adjusting sweetness or acidity if needed.
Serve hot over steamed jasmine rice or rice noodles.
